name: Process terminated
definition: Sysmon Event ID 5 records that a monitored process exited, logging its UtcTime, ProcessGuid, ProcessId, and Image path at termination.
trigger_scenarios: Sysmon logs Event ID 5 every time a monitored process ends, whether through normal completion, a user closing it, a crash, or being killed by another process or by security tooling. The event carries almost no context beyond process identity, so its value is in timing correlation rather than standalone content.

key_fields.explanation: The unique identifier for this specific process instance. Because ProcessId is reused by the OS over time, ProcessGuid is the only reliable key to join this termination back to the exact Event ID 1 creation record and any Event ID 3, 8, 10, or 11 events generated during that process's lifetime.

The executable path of the process that ended. A short-lived security or logging process (wevtutil.exe, vssadmin.exe, an EDR agent binary) terminating is more significant than a routine utility exiting.

The exact termination timestamp. A termination occurring within seconds of a file-deletion (Event ID 23/26) or event-log-clear (4719/1102) timestamp for the same host supports a deliberate cleanup sequence rather than coincidence.

false_positives: The overwhelming majority of Event ID 5 records are routine terminations of ordinary user and system processes with no security relevance.
Software updaters and installers that launch short-lived helper processes generate high volumes of this event during patch cycles.
detection_notes: Sysmon Event 5 has no direct ATT&CK technique mapping because process termination is lifecycle telemetry, not an attack behavior by itself. Investigate when the same ProcessGuid belongs to an image under C:\Users\ or C:\Temp\ and terminates within threshold=60 seconds of Event ID 23/26 file deletion, Event 1102 log clearing, or security-tool disruption; the 60-second threshold is a tunable local correlation window.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 5
| join kind=inner (Sysmon | where EventID in (23, 26)) on ProcessGuid
| where abs(datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, TimeGenerated1)) < 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ProcessGuid, TargetFilename
spl_snippet:
index=sysmon EventCode=5
| join ProcessGuid [search index=sysmon EventCode IN (23,26)]
| eval gap=abs(_time-_time1)
| where gap<10
| table _time, host, Image, ProcessGuid, TargetFilename, gap

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 5
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 5
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 5
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=5
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the event-specific field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 5
assumptions: Confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 5
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 5
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 5
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the documented field conditions and correlations in the destination SIEM.

value_references.security_relevance: Use EventID together with the other documented event fields and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: Stable process-lifetime key used to join the termination to Event 1 and related activity.
value_references.security_relevance: Use ProcessGuid together with the other documented event fields and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 5 mean?
faqs.answer: A monitored process terminated and Sysmon recorded its time and process identifiers.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 5 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. It is process-lifecycle telemetry and carries no malicious classification by itself.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 5 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Join ProcessGuid to Event 1, calculate runtime, and correlate nearby file deletion, log clearing, or security-tool disruption.
